channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sexual violence used as a weapon in Sudan’s ongoing conflict
item.description: A report from Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF) has revealed that sexual violence is being systematically used as a weapon of war in Sudan, particularly in the Darfur region. Between January 2024 and Nov...

item.title: Australia’s fuel supply secure for 39 days amid rising diesel prices
item.description: Diesel prices in Australia have surged sharply due to escalating conflict in the Middle East, with a single-day jump of $46 per barrel, the largest on record. This has pushed diesel prices to an avera...

item.title: Moderate 3.0 Magnitude Earthquake Occurs in Karbi Anglong, Assam
item.description: On Saturday evening, a minor earthquake with a magnitude of 3.0 shook the northeastern state of Assam, according to the National Center for Seismology. The tremor occurred at 8:23 pm IST and originate...

item.title: Canadian Astronaut Jeremy Hansen Connects with Earth During Artemis II Mission
item.description: Canadian astronaut Colonel Jeremy Hansen made history today by speaking live from deep space in a first for the Canadian Space Agency (CSA). As part of the Artemis II mission, Hansen connected with Ea...

item.title: Iran Ensures Safe Passage for Ships from Friendly Nations During Ongoing Conflict
item.description: Amid the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, Iran’s ambassador to India, Dr Mohammad Fathali, clarified Iran’s stance on maritime safety and wartime measures in an interview with the Times of India. ...

item.title: Queensland debates criminalising political slogans amid rising antisemitism
item.description: The state of Queensland is facing controversy over its proposed legal response to an increase in antisemitism. Instead of focusing on broader education or community initiatives, the government has tar...

item.title: Radio Skylab Returns with Special Episode on Artemis II Moon Mission
item.description: After a long hiatus, the popular podcast Radio Skylab has returned with a special episode covering the Artemis II mission, marking humanity's first crewed journey to the Moon in over 50 years. Hosted ...

item.title: The U.S. Empire’s Actions Are Increasingly Transparent
item.description: The article highlights how the actions of the United States and its allies, previously obscured by propaganda and misinformation, are now visible to the public. Caitlin Johnstone argues that what was ...

item.title: Drone crashes in UP village during private trial, no casualties reported
item.description: On Saturday, a drone being tested by a private company crashed in an agricultural field in Nandpur village, Saifai, Uttar Pradesh. The incident caused panic among locals, many of whom initially feared...

item.title: U.S. conducts search for missing pilot after Iranian forces down two aircraft
item.description: The U.S. military is intensifying efforts to locate a missing pilot following the downing of an American F-15E Strike Eagle over Iranian territory, marking the first such loss in the ongoing conflict ...

item.title: Exploring Power, Memory, and Colonial Legacies in Nadia Davids’ Cape Fever
item.description: Cape Fever is a deeply atmospheric novel by Nadia Davids, set in an unnamed harbour city reminiscent of Cape Town during the 1920s. The story follows Soraya Matas, a young Muslim woman who becomes a l...

item.title: Reflections on Choosing Parenthood: Love, Timing, and Partnership
item.description: In this deeply personal essay, Hadiya Roderique explores her decision not to have children, reflecting on love, timing, and partnership. She recounts how, after moving in with her partner at age 40, s...

item.title: Navigating Infertility in Canada: A Personal IVF Journey and Systemic Gaps
item.description: Kathryn Blaze Baum recounts her family’s arduous journey with infertility and in vitro fertilization (IVF) in Canada. After conceiving her first daughter naturally, she experienced a miscarriage in 20...

item.title: Government Plans Early Market Closures Amid Energy Crisis, Keeps Restaurants Open Till 10pm
item.description: Federal Minister for Petroleum Ali Pervaiz Malik informed Geo News that the Pakistani government is contemplating early closure of markets as part of austerity measures due to the ongoing global energ...

item.title: Bikaner Namkeen Exports Disrupted Due to US-Iran Conflict
item.description: The ongoing US-Iran conflict is causing serious concerns for traders in Bikaner, especially those involved in exporting popular snacks, spices, and namkeen to Gulf and European countries. Exporters re...

item.title: Trump’s Escalation in Iran War Increases Global Economic Strain
item.description: As President Donald Trump reinforced the U.S. stance in the ongoing conflict with Iran, global markets reacted with caution. The prolonged war has caused disruptions in oil and gas supplies, leading t...

item.title: Grey's Anatomy Episode 16 Recap: Bailey Faces Consequences and Relationships Are Tested
item.description: In the April 2 episode of 'Grey's Anatomy,' Dr. Miranda Bailey finds herself in a serious predicament after covering up an FDA-prohibited experimental treatment administered by Kwan to a patient. Init...

item.title: Tiger Woods arrested for DUI following rollover crash, cites prescription medication
item.description: Golf legend Tiger Woods was arrested for driving under the influence after a rollover accident in Jupiter Island, Florida, on Friday afternoon. His Land Rover SUV collided with the rear of a truck and...

item.title: Major Companies Shift Operations from New York to Sun Belt States Amid Tax and Cost Pressures
item.description: In early 2026, New York City experienced a significant outflow of major corporations and financial firms, with nearly 5,000 businesses relocating or expanding to lower-tax states such as Florida and T...

item.title: 5.9-Magnitude Earthquake in Afghanistan Kills 8; Tremors Felt Across Northern India
item.description: On Friday evening, a 5.9-magnitude earthquake struck Afghanistan's Hindu Kush region, resulting in the deaths of at least eight people and injuring a child in Kabul after a house collapsed. The seismi...
